zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Harcourt於1274年11月1日出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰時，他的父親Richard de Harcourt18歲，母親Margaret Beke20歲。他和Eleanor la Zouche至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1330年5月2日在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Harcourt was born on 1 November 1274, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Richard de Harcourt, was 18 and his mother, Margaret Beke, was 20. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Eleanor la Zouche. He died on 2 May 1330, in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Harcourt est né le 1 novembre 1274, à Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Harcourt, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Margaret Beke, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Eleanor la Zouche. Il est décédé le 2 mai 1330, à Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: John de Harcourt(이)가 1274년 11월 1일 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Harcourt(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Beke(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Eleanor la Zouche(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1330년 5월 2일 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.
